page: Amazon Web Services m8g.12xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor. Utilizing the ARM64 architecture, this instance provides 48 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz, with a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio. It features 192.0 GB of system memory, translating to 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The CPU cache configuration includes 64 KB of L1D cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. The instance operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 22 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the m8g.12xlarge demonstrates strong multi-core and memory subsystem performance. It achieves top-tier results in Geekbench multi-core testing, software compilation with Clang, and Passmark database operations. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with both large-block read and write operations scoring in the top 10%. Multi-threaded Bzip3 decompression and static web serving throughput also reach top-tier levels. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling in the average category. Additionally, while medium-model text generation is strong, small-model text generation is relatively weak, landing in the bottom 25%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64-based Graviton architecture delivers high resource density and exceptional multi-core throughput per unit of compute resource. This makes the instance highly competitive for parallelized workloads without the overhead of hyperthreading. The server is well-suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, database operations, static web serving, and medium-scale language model inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m8g.12xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose instance powered by a 48-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 192.0 GB of RAM, a baseline network bandwidth of 22 Gbps, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor without local storage or GPU accelerators. The server delivers top-tier performance in multi-core benchmarks, memory bandwidth, software compilation, and database operations, though single-core performance remains average. Its dedicated physical cores and high memory throughput offer excellent resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for parallelized workloads. The m8g.12xlarge is highly suited for multi-threaded applications, web serving, database management, and medium-scale LLM inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8g.12xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose instance featuring a 48-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor, 192 GB of RAM, and 22 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8g.12xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose instance with 48 dedicated Graviton cores, 192 GB RAM, and 22 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: An ARM64 general-purpose instance delivering top-tier multi-core performance and high memory bandwidth for parallelized enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 48-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 ARM64 processor at 2.8 GHz
Equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory and 22 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Top-tier performance in multi-core benchmarks, memory bandwidth, and database operations
Average single-core performance and weak small-model text generation speeds
Optimized for software compilation, web serving, and multi-threaded enterprise workloads
